meta.title: Our Recipe Library
meta.subtitle: Every recipe we’ve built. All gluten-free. All adaptable to Meg’s low glycemic needs.
meta.sweetenerNote: Wherever a recipe below calls for honey, maple syrup, or allulose, substitute monk fruit 1:1 in its place. This applies across the entire library going forward.

recipes.name: Thai Peanut Chicken Lettuce Wraps
recipes.description: Ground chicken in a sesame-peanut sauce, served in butter lettuce cups. Tested and confirmed household favorite.
recipes.tagline: Quick, low-carb, and gluten-free. A confirmed household favorite.

instructions.title: Make the peanut sauce
instructions.body: Whisk together peanut butter, tamari, sesame oil, rice vinegar, lime juice, and monk fruit sweetener. Add sriracha if using.

instructions.title: Cook the chicken
instructions.body: Heat avocado oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add garlic and ginger, cook 30 seconds. Add chicken, cook until no pink remains.

instructions.title: Add the sauce
instructions.body: Pour the sauce over the chicken and stir to coat. Cook 1-2 minutes until absorbed and slightly caramelized.

instructions.title: Assemble and serve
instructions.body: Spoon into lettuce leaves. Top with carrots, green onions, cilantro, and crushed peanuts. Serve with lime wedges.

recipes.notes: Butter lettuce works best as cups. Use gluten-free soy sauce to keep this GF.

recipes.name: Korean Beef Lettuce Wraps
recipes.description: Garlicky ground beef glazed in a sesame-ginger sauce. GF gochujang required.
recipes.tagline: Garlicky ground beef glazed in a sesame-ginger sauce, served in crisp lettuce cups.

instructions.title: Mix the sauce
instructions.body: Whisk tamari, sesame oil, rice vinegar, gochujang, monk fruit sweetener, and black pepper.

instructions.title: Brown the beef
instructions.body: Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add garlic and ginger, cook 30 seconds. Add beef, cook until browned through, draining excess fat if needed.

instructions.title: Add sauce and finish
instructions.body: Pour sauce over the beef, stir to coat, cook 2 minutes until glazed. Stir in green onions and sesame seeds.

instructions.title: Assemble
instructions.body: Spoon into lettuce cups and serve with toppings of choice.

recipes.notes: Standard gochujang contains wheat. Look for Bibigo GF Gochujang or Mother-in-Law's brand specifically.

recipes.name: Mexican Taco Lettuce Wraps
recipes.description: Ground beef or chicken with a homemade GF taco seasoning blend, served in lettuce cups with a full topping bar.
recipes.tagline: All the flavor of taco night in a crisp lettuce cup. Works with chicken or beef.

instructions.title: Season the meat
instructions.body: Toss the ground meat with the full spice blend until evenly coated.

instructions.title: Cook
instructions.body: Heat oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Add garlic, cook 30 seconds. Add seasoned meat, cook until done, breaking it apart.

instructions.title: Add liquid and finish
instructions.body: Add broth and lime juice. Cook 2 minutes until absorbed and well glazed.

instructions.title: Assemble
instructions.body: Spoon into lettuce cups and load with toppings.

recipes.notes: Skip packet taco seasoning, most contain gluten fillers. This homemade blend is fully GF.

recipes.name: Thai Peanut Pulled Pork (Instant Pot)
recipes.description: Pork shoulder braised in peanut-ginger-lime sauce in the Instant Pot. Pressure cook 60 minutes, shred, finish with lime.
recipes.tagline: Tender shredded pork shoulder in a bold sesame-peanut sauce. Great in lettuce wraps, over rice, or on its own.

ingredients.item: pork shoulder or butt, cut into chunks

instructions.title: Season and sear
instructions.body: Season pork with salt and pepper. Sear in batches in the Instant Pot on Sauté mode, 2-3 min per side. Remove and set aside.

instructions.title: Make the sauce
instructions.body: Whisk peanut butter, tamari, sesame oil, rice vinegar, monk fruit, garlic, ginger, sriracha, and fish sauce until smooth.

instructions.title: Pressure cook
instructions.body: Add broth to deglaze the pot. Return pork, pour sauce over top. Seal and cook on High Pressure 60 minutes.

instructions.title: Natural release
instructions.body: Let pressure release naturally 15 minutes, then vent any remaining steam.

instructions.title: Shred and finish
instructions.body: Shred pork with two forks. Skim excess fat, stir in lime juice, return pork to sauce.

instructions.title: Reduce if needed
instructions.body: Simmer uncovered on Sauté mode 5-8 minutes if sauce needs to thicken.

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.body: Pile into lettuce cups or over rice. Top with green onions, cilantro, and crushed peanuts.

recipes.notes: Use pork shoulder or butt only, not loin which will dry out. Fish sauce adds essential depth, don't skip it.

recipes.name: Instant Pot Teriyaki Chuck Roast
recipes.description: Boneless chuck roast seasoned with Spiceology Teriyaki Takeout, seared, then pressure cooked until tender.
recipes.tagline: Simple, bold, and tender. The Spiceology Teriyaki Takeout blend does all the heavy lifting.

instructions.title: Season the roast
instructions.body: Pat roast dry, coat all sides generously with seasoning, pressing it in.

instructions.title: Sear
instructions.body: Heat oil in Instant Pot on Sauté mode. Sear roast 3-4 min per side until deep brown crust forms. Remove.

instructions.title: Deglaze
instructions.body: Pour in broth, scrape up browned bits. Add garlic. Return roast to pot.

instructions.title: Pressure cook
instructions.body: Seal and cook on High Pressure 75 minutes for a 3 lb roast, adding 10 minutes per additional pound.

instructions.title: Natural release
instructions.body: Let pressure release naturally at least 20 minutes before venting.

instructions.title: Rest and serve
instructions.body: Rest 5-10 minutes before slicing. Spoon pan juices over the top.

recipes.notes: Chuck roast is the right cut, leaner cuts will turn out dry. For pulled texture, add 15-20 minutes to the cook time.

recipes.name: Garlic Butter Shrimp over Arugula Salad
recipes.description: Seared shrimp finished in garlic butter and chili flakes over a lemon parmesan arugula salad. Ready in 15 minutes.
recipes.tagline: Garlicky, slightly spicy shrimp over a bright lemony arugula salad. Done in 15 minutes.

instructions.title: Make the dressing
instructions.body: Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, dijon, monk fruit sweetener, and salt. Set aside.

instructions.title: Prep the shrimp
instructions.body: Pat shrimp very dry. Season with salt and pepper.

instructions.title: Sear the shrimp
instructions.body: Heat olive oil in a skillet over high heat. Cook shrimp 90 seconds without moving, flip, cook 60 seconds more.

instructions.title: Add garlic butter
instructions.body: Reduce heat, add butter, garlic, and chili flakes. Toss shrimp 60 seconds. Squeeze lemon juice over and remove from heat.

instructions.title: Assemble and serve
instructions.body: Toss arugula and tomatoes with dressing, top with parmesan, pile shrimp on top.

recipes.notes: Pat shrimp very dry before cooking. Dress the arugula just before serving, it wilts quickly.

recipes.name: Chicken and Zucchini Cabbage Rolls
recipes.description: Ground chicken and finely diced zucchini wrapped in napa cabbage, pan-fried, served with a sesame dipping sauce. Zucchini stands in for mushrooms.
recipes.tagline: Savory ground chicken and zucchini wrapped in tender napa cabbage, pan-fried golden, served with a sesame dipping sauce.

instructions.title: Cook the zucchini
instructions.body: Heat oil in a skillet, add zucchini with a pinch of salt, cook until moisture releases and it begins to brown, about 5 minutes.

instructions.title: Build the filling
instructions.body: Add white parts of green onion and garlic, cook 1 minute. Season with pepper, ginger powder, soy sauce. Add chicken, cook until done.

instructions.title: Prepare the cabbage
instructions.body: Boil cabbage leaves 5-10 minutes until tender and pliable. Pat very dry.

instructions.title: Make the sauce
instructions.body: Whisk soy sauce, monk fruit sweetener, rice vinegar, sesame oil, garlic powder, and green onion tops.

instructions.title: Assemble the rolls
instructions.body: Place filling near the base of each leaf, fold sides in, roll up firmly.

instructions.title: Pan-fry
instructions.body: Brown rolls seam-side down first, then all sides, about 6-8 minutes total.

instructions.title: Sauce and serve
instructions.body: Serve hot with sauce poured over, garnished with sesame seeds and green onion.

recipes.notes: Zucchini replaces mushrooms here for those who don't like them, doing the same job of releasing moisture into the filling.

recipes.name: Grilled Steak
recipes.description: Grilled flank or strip steak, seasoned simply, seared over high heat, and rested before slicing thin against the grain.
recipes.tagline: The steak night that opens the rotation. Buy it the day you cook it.

instructions.title: Season
instructions.body: Pat the steak dry, coat lightly with oil, and season both sides with salt, pepper, and garlic powder. Let it sit 20 to 30 minutes at room temperature.

instructions.title: Heat the grill
instructions.body: Bring one zone of the grill to high heat. Clean and oil the grate.

instructions.title: Sear
instructions.body: Grill 4 to 5 minutes per side for medium-rare flank, or to your preferred doneness. Strip may need an extra minute per side depending on thickness.

instructions.title: Rest
instructions.body: Move to a board, top with butter if using, tent loosely, and rest 10 minutes. Do not skip this.

instructions.title: Slice
instructions.body: Slice thin against the grain and serve.

recipes.notes: Flank is the more affordable cut and slices beautifully; strip is more forgiving on doneness. Pairs well with blistered cherry tomatoes or a side salad. Any leftovers slice cold into the Thai-Style Steak Salad the next day.

recipes.name: Tuscan Shrimp
recipes.description: Seared shrimp in a garlic sun-dried tomato cream sauce with baby kale and parmesan. Rich, fast, and naturally gluten-free.
recipes.tagline: A quick seafood night in the Marry Me family of flavors.

instructions.title: Season the shrimp
instructions.body: Pat dry and season with salt and pepper.

instructions.title: Sear
instructions.body: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high. Sear shrimp 1 to 2 minutes per side until just pink. Remove to a plate.

instructions.title: Build the sauce
instructions.body: Lower heat to medium, add butter, garlic, and sun-dried tomatoes, and cook 1 minute. Pour in the broth and scrape up the bottom of the pan.

instructions.title: Cream it
instructions.body: Add cream, parmesan, Italian seasoning, and chili flakes. Simmer 3 to 4 minutes until slightly thickened.

instructions.title: Finish
instructions.body: Stir in the baby kale until wilted, return the shrimp and any juices, and warm through for 1 minute.

recipes.notes: S skips shrimp, so sear about 1½ lb sliced chicken breast the same way for her portion, or set aside sauce and baby kale before the shrimp goes in. For Meg, serve over cauliflower rice to keep it low glycemic; the girls can have rice or GF pasta. Holds and reheats gently over low heat with a splash of broth.

recipes.name: Egg Roll in a Bowl
recipes.description: All the flavor of an egg roll with none of the wrapper. Ground meat and cabbage in a savory ginger-garlic sauce, ready in about 20 minutes.
recipes.tagline: The cabbage does the heavy lifting on the vegetable side.

instructions.title: Brown the meat
instructions.body: Heat sesame oil in a large skillet over medium-high. Add the ground meat and cook until browned, breaking it up.

instructions.title: Aromatics
instructions.body: Add garlic and ginger and cook 1 minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Cabbage
instructions.body: Add the coleslaw mix and toss 3 to 4 minutes until it wilts but keeps some crunch.

instructions.title: Sauce
instructions.body: Stir in tamari, rice vinegar, and monk fruit; cook 2 minutes until the cabbage is coated and glossy.

instructions.title: Finish
instructions.body: Top with green onions, sesame seeds, and sriracha if using.

recipes.notes: The cabbage is the vegetable here, so no separate side is needed. The only gluten risk is soy sauce, so use a gluten-free one. Reheats very well, making it a strong late-plate option for Meg.

recipes.name: Grilled Pork Sirloin Chops
recipes.description: Boneless pork sirloin chops in a smoky paprika-cumin marinade, grilled hot and fast, with asparagus cooked right alongside.
recipes.tagline: A from-scratch marinade so there is no hidden gluten.

instructions.title: Marinade
instructions.body: Whisk olive oil, vinegar, smoked paprika, cumin, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Coat the chops and marinate at least 30 minutes, up to 4 hours.

instructions.title: Heat the grill
instructions.body: Medium-high heat. Clean and oil the grate.

instructions.title: Grill
instructions.body: Cook the chops 4 to 5 minutes per side until 145°F internal. Grill asparagus alongside for the last 3 to 4 minutes, turning once.

instructions.title: Rest
instructions.body: Rest the chops 5 minutes before serving.

recipes.notes: Apple cider vinegar keeps this GF; bottled marinades are a common hidden-gluten spot, which is why this one is from scratch. Pork sirloin is lean and dries out past 145°F, so pull it on time. Prosciutto-wrapped asparagus goes right on the grate with no second pan.

recipes.name: Butter Chicken
recipes.description: Chicken simmered in a spiced tomato-cream sauce. Deeply flavored, gluten-free, and easy to make low glycemic.
recipes.tagline: Better the next day, and it reheats beautifully.

instructions.title: Marinate
instructions.body: Toss the chicken with yogurt, 1 tsp of the garam masala, and turmeric. Let sit 20 minutes, or overnight.

instructions.title: Sear
instructions.body: Melt 1 tbsp butter in a large skillet over medium-high and brown the chicken in batches. Remove.

instructions.title: Sauce base
instructions.body: Add the remaining butter, onion, garlic, and ginger; cook 4 to 5 minutes until soft. Stir in tomato paste, the remaining garam masala, cumin, and paprika; cook 1 minute.

instructions.title: Simmer
instructions.body: Add crushed tomatoes and salt; simmer 10 minutes. For a smooth sauce, blend with an immersion blender.

instructions.title: Finish
instructions.body: Stir in cream and monk fruit, return the chicken, and simmer 8 to 10 minutes until cooked through.

recipes.notes: Naturally GF as written; the watch-out is store-bought curry paste, which can hide gluten. For Meg, serve over cauliflower rice to keep it low glycemic; the girls can have basmati rice.

recipes.name: Smash Burgers
recipes.description: Thin, craggy, deeply seared beef patties done bunless over lettuce. Fast, low carb, and better than the drive-thru.
recipes.tagline: Bunless holds together better than any GF bun.

instructions.title: Portion
instructions.body: Divide the beef into 4 to 8 loose balls. Do not season yet or pack them tight.

instructions.title: Heat cast iron
instructions.body: Get a cast iron skillet or griddle screaming hot.

instructions.title: Smash
instructions.body: Place a ball down, smash flat and hard with a spatula, and season the top. Sear 2 to 3 minutes until the edges are lacy and browned.

instructions.title: Flip and cheese
instructions.body: Flip, add cheese, and cook 1 minute more.

instructions.title: Build
instructions.body: Serve over butter lettuce with tomato, onion, Dijon, and pickles.

recipes.notes: Bunless is the better call here; GF buns tend to fall apart under smash burgers, and lettuce actually holds together better. The girls can add a GF bun or a side of fries. Check that pickles and any special sauce are GF.

recipes.name: Grilled Chicken Breast
recipes.description: Reliable grilled chicken in a bright lemon-oregano marinade that keeps it from drying out. Slice against the grain before serving.
recipes.tagline: Simple and dependable, with asparagus on the grate alongside.

instructions.title: Marinade
instructions.body: Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, oregano, salt, and pepper. Add the chicken and marinate at least 30 minutes, up to overnight.

instructions.title: Even thickness
instructions.body: Pound thicker breasts to an even thickness so they cook evenly.

instructions.title: Grill
instructions.body: Medium-high heat, 5 to 6 minutes per side until 165°F internal.

instructions.title: Rest and slice
instructions.body: Rest 5 minutes, then slice against the grain.

recipes.notes: Use organic, no-hormone chicken. Prosciutto-wrapped asparagus grills alongside for a no-extra-pan vegetable. Leftover sliced chicken is great cold over greens the next day.

recipes.name: Tuscan Chicken
recipes.description: Seared chicken in a garlic sun-dried tomato cream sauce with baby kale and parmesan. Big flavor for low effort.
recipes.tagline: Same Marry Me profile as the Tuscan Shrimp, spaced two weeks apart.

ingredients.item: boneless skinless chicken breasts or thighs

instructions.title: Season and sear
instructions.body: Season the chicken with salt and pepper. Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high and sear 4 to 5 minutes per side until golden and cooked through. Remove.

instructions.title: Aromatics
instructions.body: Lower to medium, add butter, garlic, and sun-dried tomatoes, and cook 1 minute.

instructions.title: Deglaze
instructions.body: Add the broth and scrape the bottom of the pan.

instructions.title: Cream sauce
instructions.body: Stir in cream, parmesan, Italian seasoning, and chili flakes; simmer 3 to 4 minutes to thicken.

instructions.title: Finish
instructions.body: Wilt in the baby kale, return the chicken and juices, and warm through.

recipes.notes: Spaced two weeks from the Tuscan Shrimp, this shares the flavor profile but eats completely differently. Naturally GF and low carb; the girls can add pasta or bread. Reheats well over low heat with a splash of broth.

recipes.name: Shrimp Stir Fry
recipes.description: The fastest meal in the rotation, around 15 minutes. Shrimp and crisp vegetables in a quick ginger-garlic tamari sauce.
recipes.tagline: Vegetables built in, on the table in fifteen minutes.

instructions.title: Sauce
instructions.body: Whisk tamari, rice vinegar, sesame oil, monk fruit, and the cornstarch if using.

instructions.title: Sear the shrimp
instructions.body: Heat 1 tbsp avocado oil in a large skillet or wok over high. Cook shrimp 1 to 2 minutes per side until just pink. Remove.

instructions.title: Vegetables
instructions.body: Add the remaining oil, then the snap peas and peppers; stir-fry 3 to 4 minutes until crisp-tender.

instructions.title: Aromatics
instructions.body: Add garlic and ginger and stir 30 seconds.

instructions.title: Combine
instructions.body: Return the shrimp, pour in the sauce, and toss 1 minute until glossy. Finish with green onions and sesame seeds.

recipes.notes: S skips shrimp, so swap in thin-sliced chicken or extra vegetables for her portion. Meg eats it as is for low glycemic; the girls can add rice. Leave out the cornstarch to keep it strictly low glycemic.

recipes.name: Grilled Chicken Wings
recipes.description: Wings done low and slow over indirect heat, then crisped over direct heat. A GF dry rub does all the work.
recipes.tagline: The grill beats the oven on wings every time.

instructions.title: Dry the wings
instructions.body: Pat the wings very dry. This is the key to crisp skin.

instructions.title: Rub
instructions.body: Toss with smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, cayenne, and baking powder.

instructions.title: Indirect cook
instructions.body: Set the grill for indirect heat at about 375°F. Cook the wings on the cool side 25 minutes, turning once.

instructions.title: Crisp
instructions.body: Move to direct high heat 3 to 4 minutes per side until the skin is crisp and browned.

recipes.notes: Baking powder, not baking soda, and make sure it is GF; it helps the skin crisp and can be left out. Pairs with roasted broccoli. Serve any sauce on the side rather than tossed to keep them crisp, and check bottled wing sauce for gluten.

recipes.name: Keto Korean Beef Bowls
recipes.description: Ground beef in a sweet-savory gochujang sauce, served over cauliflower rice or regular rice. Weeknight fast.
recipes.tagline: Sweetened with monk fruit, built on GF gochujang.

ingredients.item: cauliflower rice (or steamed rice for the girls)

instructions.title: Sauce
instructions.body: Whisk tamari, gochujang, monk fruit, and rice vinegar.

instructions.title: Brown the beef
instructions.body: Heat sesame oil in a skillet over medium-high and brown the beef, breaking it up. Drain excess fat.

instructions.title: Aromatics
instructions.body: Add garlic and ginger and cook 1 minute.

instructions.title: Glaze
instructions.body: Pour in the sauce and simmer 3 to 4 minutes until it reduces and coats the beef.

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.body: Spoon over cauliflower rice, or rice for the girls, and top with green onions and sesame seeds.

recipes.notes: Use Bibigo or Mother-in-Law's gochujang, which are GF; most gochujang contains wheat, so this is easy to get wrong at the store. Brown sugar is the usual sweetener, replaced here with monk fruit. Cauliflower rice keeps it low glycemic for Meg. Reheats well.

recipes.name: Crispy Chicken
recipes.description: Crunchy pan-fried chicken cutlets with a gluten-free crust that actually stays crisp.
recipes.tagline: A classic hidden-gluten trap, made GF and still crunchy.

ingredients.item: boneless skinless chicken breasts, halved into cutlets
crushed pork rinds or GF panko

instructions.title: Prep cutlets
instructions.body: Slice the breasts into even cutlets, pat dry, and season with salt and pepper.

instructions.title: Breading station
instructions.body: Set up three dishes: GF flour; beaten eggs; and pork rinds or GF panko mixed with parmesan, garlic powder, and paprika.

instructions.title: Bread
instructions.body: Dredge each cutlet in flour, then egg, then press firmly into the crumb mixture.

instructions.title: Fry
instructions.body: Heat avocado oil in a skillet over medium-high. Fry 3 to 4 minutes per side until deep golden and 165°F internal.

instructions.title: Drain
instructions.body: Rest on a wire rack, not paper towels, to stay crisp.

recipes.notes: Crispy chicken is a classic hidden-gluten trap, so this uses a GF flour blend and either crushed pork rinds or GF panko. Pork rinds keep it low glycemic for Meg and get the crispiest result; GF panko is lighter. The girls can have theirs over pasta or on GF bread. The pork-rind-and-parmesan crust is my choice here for the GF crunch, so adjust to taste.

recipes.name: Sheet Pan Sausage & Zucchini
recipes.description: Everything roasted on one pan: GF sausage, zucchini, peppers, and onion. Summer produce, minimal cleanup.
recipes.tagline: The zucchini is built in, the way cabbage carries the egg roll bowl.

instructions.title: Heat the oven
instructions.body: Preheat to 425°F and line a sheet pan.

instructions.title: Toss
instructions.body: Combine zucchini, peppers, onion, and tomatoes with olive oil, Italian seasoning, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Spread on the pan.

instructions.title: Add sausage
instructions.body: Nestle the sausage, whole links or sliced, among the vegetables.

instructions.title: Roast
instructions.body: Roast 25 to 30 minutes, tossing once, until the sausage is cooked through and the vegetables are browned at the edges.

recipes.notes: Check the sausage label; some contain wheat or fillers, so buy a GF-labeled sausage. The zucchini and peppers are the vegetable, built into the dish. Naturally low glycemic, and it holds and reheats well for a late plate.

recipes.name: Thai Style Chicken Satay
recipes.description: Add a Thai red curry twist to everyone's favourite chicken satay.
recipes.tagline: Add a Thai red curry twist to everyone's favourite chicken satay.

instructions.title: Make the base
instructions.body: In a large bowl, combine the peanut butter, Thai red curry paste, tamari, and 50 ml of the coconut milk. Beat until smooth.

instructions.title: Split off the sauce
instructions.body: Transfer two-thirds of the mixture into a saucepan, add the remaining coconut milk, and mix well. This becomes the satay sauce; the third left in the bowl is the marinade.

instructions.title: Marinate the chicken
instructions.body: Cut the chicken into bite-sized pieces, add to the bowl with the remaining marinade, and mix until completely coated.

instructions.title: Skewer
instructions.body: Thread the marinated chicken onto 4 skewers.

instructions.title: Cook the chicken
instructions.body: Heat some oil in a large pan and cook the chicken over medium heat until golden brown all over and cooked through.

instructions.title: Finish the sauce
instructions.body: Heat the reserved sauce, taste for seasoning, then stir in the chopped parsley.

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.body: Plate the skewers with rice and pour over the satay sauce.

recipes.notes: Use a GF-labeled Thai red curry paste, and note that most red curry pastes contain shrimp paste, which matters for S. Amounts are as written on the source, in metric. For Meg, serve over cauliflower rice to keep it low glycemic; the girls can have regular rice. Times: 10 min prep, 15 min cook, 25 min total. Cuisine: Thai.

recipes.name: Balsamic Kielbasa and Kidney Beans
recipes.description: Smoky kielbasa simmered with kidney beans in balsamic-spiked tomato sauce. Serve over rice, GF pasta, or roasted broccoli.
recipes.tagline: A fast, hearty skillet built on the house Pasta Sauce.

ingredients.item: red kidney beans, drained and rinsed

instructions.title: Heat the oil
instructions.body: Heat the olive oil in a large pan over medium heat.

instructions.title: Brown the kielbasa
instructions.body: Add the kielbasa and brown it, stirring occasionally.

instructions.title: Soften the aromatics
instructions.body: Add the shallot and garlic and cook until softened, a couple of minutes.

instructions.title: Build the pot
instructions.body: Stir in the balsamic vinegar, Pasta Sauce, kidney beans, thyme, and salt.

instructions.title: Simmer
instructions.body: Simmer for 10 minutes.

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.body: Serve over rice, GF pasta, or roasted broccoli.

recipes.notes: Uses 3 cups of the {{r:pasta-sauce|Pasta Sauce}} recipe. Use a GF-labeled kielbasa, since some contain wheat fillers. If using dried thyme instead of fresh, start with about 1 teaspoon. The beans are drained and rinsed here; leave the liquid in if you want it looser.

recipes.name: Instant Pot Chicken Enchilada Soup
recipes.description: A quick, keto Instant Pot chicken enchilada soup that makes a great weeknight dinner.
recipes.tagline: Ready start to finish in about 30 minutes.

ingredients.item: sea salt or pink Himalayan salt
diced avocado, sliced jalapeño, sour cream, or minced cilantro (optional toppings)

instructions.title: Sauté the vegetables
instructions.body: Drizzle the olive oil into the Instant Pot and set to Sauté. Add the onion, garlic, bell pepper, and jalapeño. Cook 3 to 4 minutes until soft.

instructions.title: Mix the sauce
instructions.body: In a small bowl, stir together the tomato sauce, vinegar, chipotle, and spices. Pour into the pot.

instructions.title: Pressure cook
instructions.body: Add the broth and chicken and stir. Lock the lid and set to Manual high pressure for 20 minutes, then release the vent valve.

instructions.title: Shred
instructions.body: Remove the chicken, shred it, return it to the pot, and stir.

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.body: Top with any of the optional toppings.

instructions.title: Store
instructions.body: Enjoy hot, or cool and store covered in the refrigerator for up to 4 days.

recipes.notes: Keto and low carb. About 10 minutes hands-on, 30 minutes total, 275 kcal per serving. Use GF-labeled chili powder and chipotle in adobo, since spice blends and canned sauces can contain gluten.

recipes.name: Thai-Inspired Chicken Meatball Soup
recipes.description: Ginger chicken meatballs simmered in a coconut milk broth with baby kale and lime.
recipes.tagline: A cozy one-pot soup, ready in about 30 minutes.

ingredients.item: cilantro, leaves and stems finely chopped, a few leaves reserved
vegetable or coconut oil, plus more as needed
lime juice, plus lime wedges for serving
steamed white or brown rice, for serving

instructions.title: Make the meatball mix
instructions.body: Grate the ginger, garlic, and jalapeño on the small holes of a box grater or a Microplane, or finely chop by hand. Transfer half to a large bowl and set the rest aside. To the bowl add the ground chicken, chopped cilantro, 2 tbsp of the fish sauce, and 1 tsp salt. Combine gently with your hands or a fork; do not overmix.

instructions.title: Brown the meatballs
instructions.body: Form 2-inch meatballs, about 2 oz each. Heat the oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium-high. Working in batches, brown the meatballs on two sides, 5 to 8 minutes, adding oil as needed. Transfer to a plate.

instructions.title: Build the broth
instructions.body: If the oil is burned, wipe the pot and add a little fresh oil. Over medium heat, add the reserved ginger mixture and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute. Add the broth, coconut milk, monk fruit, and remaining 1 tbsp fish sauce, and bring to a simmer. Return the meatballs and their juices and simmer until cooked through, 5 to 8 minutes.

instructions.title: Finish
instructions.body: Off the heat, stir in the baby kale and lime juice. Serve over rice, topped with the reserved cilantro and lime wedges.

recipes.notes: Adapted from Ali Slagle, NYT Cooking. Sugar swapped for monk fruit. The soup is brothy, so serve it over rice to make it a full meal. Total time about 30 minutes.

recipes.name: Keto Tuscan Soup
recipes.description: A hearty, creamy low-carb zuppa toscana with sausage and veggies.
recipes.tagline: A rich, sausage-forward low-carb soup.

instructions.title: Brown the sausage
instructions.body: In a Dutch oven, brown both sausages (the hot and the sweet) in larger chunks, then drain and set aside.

instructions.title: Sauté the vegetables
instructions.body: In the same Dutch oven, add the butter, garlic, onion, and tomatoes. Sauté for 7 minutes.

instructions.title: Deglaze
instructions.body: Add about ¼ cup of the chicken broth and deglaze, scraping the browned bits from the bottom.

instructions.title: Simmer the base
instructions.body: Add the rest of the broth and the seasonings, then bring to a boil.

instructions.title: Simmer
instructions.body: Reduce to medium heat and simmer 20 to 30 minutes with the lid on to let the flavors come together. Stoves vary, so adjust the time.

instructions.title: Add cream and sausage
instructions.body: Add the sausage, heavy whipping cream, and baby kale, and simmer on low with the lid on for about 10 minutes.

instructions.title: Finish
instructions.body: Add the cheese and bacon and stir until the cheese melts.

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.body: Serve hot.

recipes.notes: Adapted from Kasey Trenum. Mushrooms from the original recipe are left out. Use GF-labeled Italian sausage. Prep 10 minutes, cook 50 minutes.

recipes.name: Thai-Style Steak Salad
recipes.description: Seared flat iron or flank steak sliced thin over greens with mint, cilantro, and a 3:1 lime-to-fish-sauce dressing. Dress within 5 minutes of serving.
recipes.tagline: Thin-sliced seared steak over a bright Thai-inspired salad with fresh herbs, crunch, and a punchy lime dressing.

instructions.title: Make the dressing
instructions.body: Whisk lime juice, fish sauce, monk fruit sweetener, garlic, chili flakes, and sesame oil until combined.

instructions.title: Prep the steak
instructions.body: Pat steak dry, season both sides with salt and pepper.

instructions.title: Sear the steak
instructions.body: Heat oil in a cast iron skillet over high heat until smoking. Sear 4-5 min per side for medium-rare.

instructions.title: Rest
instructions.body: Rest on a cutting board for 10 minutes. Do not skip this.

instructions.title: Build the salad
instructions.body: While resting, chop herbs and combine with greens, carrots, cucumber, and green onions. Hold off on dressing until just before plating.

instructions.title: Dress, slice, and serve
instructions.body: Dress gradually, tossing and stopping when lightly coated. Slice steak thin against the grain, arrange over salad, finish with peanuts.

recipes.notes: Flat iron needs a longer sear than flank. Slice against the grain always. Don't dress more than 5 minutes before serving.

recipes.name: Mixed Greens Salad with Prosciutto, Mozzarella and Peach
recipes.description: Mixed greens (no arugula or spinach), prosciutto, fresh mozzarella, peach as an accent, toasted pine nuts, lemon vinaigrette.
recipes.tagline: A bright, fresh salad with salty prosciutto, creamy fresh mozzarella, toasted pine nuts, and peach as a subtle accent.

instructions.title: Toast the pine nuts
instructions.body: Toast in a dry skillet over medium heat 2-3 minutes until golden, stirring frequently. Remove immediately.

instructions.title: Make the dressing
instructions.body: Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, dijon, salt, and pepper until emulsified.

instructions.title: Build the salad
instructions.body: Arrange greens on a platter. Tuck prosciutto, mozzarella, and peach throughout. Scatter red onion and pine nuts.

instructions.title: Dress and serve
instructions.body: Drizzle dressing gradually just before serving, tossing lightly.

recipes.notes: Half a peach keeps the fruit as an accent, not the focal point. Check bagged greens for arugula or spinach before buying.

recipes.name: Mango Slaw
recipes.description: Shredded cabbage, mango, carrots, herbs, and lime-honey dressing. Rest 10 minutes before serving for best texture.
recipes.tagline: Cool, bright, and slightly sweet. A natural partner for Thai peanut chicken wraps and grilled pork.

ingredients.item: ripe mango, julienned or diced small

instructions.title: Make the dressing
instructions.body: Whisk lime juice, rice vinegar, sesame oil, monk fruit sweetener, and salt until dissolved.

instructions.title: Combine the slaw
instructions.body: Add cabbage, mango, carrots, green onions, and cilantro to a large bowl. Add jalapeño and red pepper flakes if using.

instructions.title: Dress and toss
instructions.body: Pour dressing over slaw and toss well to coat.

instructions.title: Rest before serving
instructions.body: Let sit at least 10 minutes before serving so the cabbage softens slightly.

recipes.notes: Use a ripe but firm mango so it holds its shape. Keeps in the fridge up to 2 days.

recipes.name: Cucumber Sesame Salad
recipes.description: Thin-sliced cucumber, rice vinegar, sesame oil, green onion, sesame seeds. Ready in 10 minutes, no heat required.
recipes.tagline: Cool, crisp, and ready in 10 minutes. No heat required.

instructions.title: Prep the cucumbers
instructions.body: Slice as thin as possible. Spread on a paper towel, salt lightly, let sit 5 minutes, pat dry.

instructions.title: Make the dressing
instructions.body: Whisk rice vinegar, sesame oil, monk fruit sweetener, and salt until dissolved. Add red pepper flakes if using.

instructions.title: Toss and serve
instructions.body: Add cucumbers and green onions to the dressing, toss to coat. Scatter sesame seeds over the top.

recipes.notes: English cucumbers work best, thinner skin and fewer seeds. Can be made up to an hour ahead.

recipes.name: Kale, Apple, and Goat Cheese Salad with Granola Croutons
recipes.description: Massaged kale, apple, goat cheese, dried cranberries, and a lemon mustard dressing, topped with homemade nut and seed granola. Use certified GF oats. For Meg: skip the granola and cranberries to keep it low glycemic.
recipes.tagline: Massaged kale, crisp apple, and crumbled goat cheese tossed in a tangy lemon dressing, topped with homemade granola croutons.

instructions.title: Make the granola
instructions.body: Preheat oven to 350°F. Combine oats, pecans, pepitas, sunflower seeds, salt, cinnamon, and cayenne. Stir in egg white, oil, and monk fruit sweetener. Spread on a parchment-lined sheet pan, bake 15-17 minutes, stirring once. Cool.

instructions.title: Make the dressing
instructions.body: Combine olive oil, lemon juice, dijon, monk fruit sweetener, and salt. Whisk until blended, season with pepper.

instructions.title: Prepare the kale
instructions.body: Remove ribs, chop leaves small. Sprinkle with a pinch of salt and massage with your hands until darker and fragrant.

instructions.title: Assemble
instructions.body: Drizzle dressing over kale, toss well. Add apple, goat cheese, cranberries, and about half the granola. Toss gently and serve.

recipes.notes: For Meg: skip the granola and dried cranberries to keep this low glycemic. Use certified GF oats. Leftover granola keeps 2 weeks at room temperature.

recipes.name: Roasted Broccoli with Parmesan and Lemon
recipes.description: High-heat roasted broccoli with lemon zest before roasting, lemon juice and parmesan after.
recipes.tagline: Simple roasted broccoli with crispy edges and a bright finish. A reliable side for almost any protein.

instructions.title: Prep and season
instructions.body: Preheat oven to 425°F. Pat broccoli dry, spread on a sheet pan without crowding. Toss with oil, salt, pepper, garlic, and lemon zest.

instructions.title: Roast
instructions.body: Roast 20-22 minutes, flipping once, until edges are deeply browned and crispy.

instructions.title: Finish and serve
instructions.body: Squeeze lemon juice over the top, scatter parmesan, add red pepper flakes if using, toss and serve.

recipes.notes: Don't crowd the pan, the florets will steam instead of roast. Zest goes on before roasting, juice and parmesan go on after.

recipes.name: Blistered Cherry Tomatoes
recipes.description: Hot-pan blistered tomatoes with garlic, olive oil, and fresh basil. Ready in 5 minutes.
recipes.tagline: Blistered cherry tomatoes with garlic and olive oil. A quick, bright side that pairs well with steak and roasted broccoli.

instructions.title: Heat the pan
instructions.body: Heat olive oil in a skillet over high heat until shimmering and just starting to smoke.

instructions.title: Blister the tomatoes
instructions.body: Add tomatoes in a single layer, do not move them, cook 2-3 minutes until skins blister and char.

instructions.title: Add garlic
instructions.body: Add garlic and red pepper flakes if using. Toss once, cook 60-90 seconds more until garlic is golden.

instructions.title: Season and serve
instructions.body: Remove from heat, season with salt and pepper, scatter basil over the top, serve immediately.

recipes.notes: A very hot pan is key. Don't move the tomatoes too early or they'll steam instead of char.

recipes.name: Prosciutto Wrapped Asparagus
recipes.description: Asparagus spears wrapped in prosciutto, grilled or roasted until the prosciutto crisps. Grills alongside the chicken or pork with no extra pan.
recipes.tagline: The grill-alongside vegetable for the chicken and pork nights.

instructions.title: Trim
instructions.body: Snap or cut the woody ends off the asparagus.

instructions.title: Wrap
instructions.body: Bundle 3 to 4 spears and wrap with a slice of prosciutto. Drizzle lightly with olive oil and season with pepper. No salt needed; the prosciutto is salty.

instructions.title: Cook
instructions.body: Grill over medium-high 3 to 4 minutes, turning once, until the prosciutto crisps and the asparagus is tender. Or roast at 425°F for 12 to 15 minutes.

instructions.title: Finish
instructions.body: Squeeze lemon over the top before serving.

recipes.notes: Grills right on the grate alongside the grilled chicken breast or pork sirloin chops, no second pan. Naturally GF and low glycemic. This side is not from your rotation list by name as a recipe, but it is the paired vegetable called out on two grill nights, so I added it to round out the plan.

recipes.name: Pasta Sauce
recipes.description: A simple, chunky tomato sauce with shallot, garlic, and fresh basil. Naturally gluten-free.
recipes.tagline: Rob's everyday red sauce, adapted from Joanne Gallagher.

ingredients.item: Aleppo pepper or crushed red pepper flakes
fine sea salt, plus more to taste
fresh basil leaves, chopped, or 1 to 2 tsp dried basil

instructions.title: Sweat the shallot
instructions.body: Heat the olive oil in a large, heavy-bottomed saucepan over medium heat. Add the shallot and cook until soft, 3 to 4 minutes.

instructions.title: Bloom the aromatics
instructions.body: Stir in the garlic, pepper flakes, Italian seasoning, and tomato paste. Cook about 1 minute to develop the flavor.

instructions.title: Simmer
instructions.body: Add the canned tomatoes with their juices and break them up with a spoon or masher for a chunky sauce. Lower the heat to a gentle simmer, stir in the salt and pepper, and cook 20 to 30 minutes until reduced by about a third, tasting and adjusting as it goes.

instructions.title: Finish
instructions.body: Turn off the heat and stir in the fresh basil. Leave it chunky, or blend with a stand or immersion blender for a smoother sauce.

recipes.notes: Storing: keeps in the fridge up to 4 days, or frozen up to 3 months; thaw overnight and reheat gently. Fresh tomatoes: swap in 4 to 5 lb of ripe fresh tomatoes (peeling is optional via a quick blanch and ice bath). Balancing: if it lacks flavor add more salt; if too acidic, a pinch of monk fruit balances it (house swap for sugar), and a dash of fish sauce or minced anchovy adds savory depth. For extra richness, simmer a parmesan rind in it and remove before serving. To thicken, simmer 10 to 15 minutes longer. Adapted from Joanne Gallagher.

recipes.name: Salsa
recipes.description: A quick blended salsa with tomatillos, jalapeño, and sweet peppers. Sweetened with monk fruit.
recipes.tagline: Rob's blender salsa.

instructions.title: Prep
instructions.body: Husk and rinse the tomatillos. Roughly chop the shallot, jalapeño, and sweet peppers. Seed the jalapeño if you want it milder.

instructions.title: Blend
instructions.body: Add everything to a blender and blend to your preferred consistency. Taste and adjust the salt and monk fruit.

recipes.notes: This is a raw, blended salsa. For a cooked, mellower version, simmer the blended salsa in a saucepan for about 10 minutes, then cool. The husking and optional seeding steps were added as standard tomatillo and jalapeño prep; the rest is as written.

recipes.name: Thai Peanut Sauce
recipes.description: A quick peanut sauce with tamari, sesame, lime, and a touch of heat. Good on chicken, pork, or noodles.
recipes.tagline: The sauce from the Thai peanut chicken wraps.

instructions.title: Whisk
instructions.body: Whisk everything together until smooth. Loosen with a splash of warm water if it is too thick, and adjust the sriracha to taste.

recipes.notes: This is the sauce from Thai Peanut Chicken Lettuce Wraps. The Thai Peanut Pulled Pork uses a richer, larger-batch version of the same idea: ¼ cup peanut butter, 3 tbsp soy sauce (GF), 1 tbsp sesame oil, 2 tbsp rice vinegar, 1 tbsp monk fruit, 4 minced garlic cloves, 1 tbsp grated ginger, 1 tbsp sriracha, and 1 tbsp fish sauce, meant to be cooked with the pork rather than served raw.

recipes.name: Korean Gochujang Sauce
recipes.description: A savory-sweet gochujang glaze for ground beef, bowls, or vegetables.
recipes.tagline: The sauce from the Korean beef wraps.

instructions.title: Whisk
instructions.body: Whisk everything together until smooth. Add to browned meat and simmer 2 to 3 minutes until it glazes.

recipes.notes: Use Bibigo or Mother-in-Law's gochujang, which are gluten-free; most gochujang contains wheat. This is the sauce from Korean Beef Lettuce Wraps. The Keto Korean Beef Bowls use a larger, punchier batch: ⅓ cup soy sauce (GF), 1 tbsp gochujang, 2 tbsp monk fruit, and 1 tbsp rice vinegar.

recipes.name: Ginger Sesame Sauce
recipes.description: A light soy-sesame finishing sauce with a little sweetness and green onion.
recipes.tagline: The pour-over sauce from the chicken cabbage rolls.

instructions.title: Whisk
instructions.body: Whisk the tamari, monk fruit, rice vinegar, sesame oil, and garlic powder until the sweetener dissolves. Stir in the green onion tops.

recipes.notes: This is the finishing sauce from Chicken and Zucchini Cabbage Rolls, poured over just before serving.

recipes.name: Stir-Fry Sauce
recipes.description: A fast tamari-sesame sauce that glazes any stir-fry. Thicken with a little cornstarch if you like.
recipes.tagline: The sauce from the shrimp stir fry.

instructions.title: Whisk
instructions.body: Whisk everything together. Pour into the pan at the end of cooking and toss 1 minute until glossy.

recipes.notes: This is the sauce from Shrimp Stir Fry. Egg Roll in a Bowl uses a simpler version with no sesame oil or cornstarch: ¼ cup soy sauce (GF), 1 tbsp rice vinegar, and 1 tsp monk fruit.

recipes.name: Tuscan Cream Sauce
recipes.description: A garlic sun-dried tomato cream sauce with parmesan and baby kale. The Marry Me sauce.
recipes.tagline: The identical sauce from the Tuscan Shrimp and Tuscan Chicken.

instructions.title: Aromatics
instructions.body: Melt the butter in a skillet over medium heat. Add the garlic and sun-dried tomatoes and cook 1 minute.

instructions.title: Deglaze
instructions.body: Pour in the broth and scrape up the bottom of the pan.

instructions.title: Cream
instructions.body: Stir in the cream, parmesan, Italian seasoning, and chili flakes; simmer 3 to 4 minutes to thicken.

instructions.title: Finish
instructions.body: Wilt in the baby kale. Spoon over any seared protein, or toss with pasta or cauliflower rice.

recipes.notes: This is the exact sauce used in both Tuscan Shrimp and Tuscan Chicken. For Meg, serve it over cauliflower rice to keep it low glycemic; the girls can have it over pasta.

recipes.name: Butter Chicken Sauce
recipes.description: A spiced tomato-cream makhani sauce. Simmer any protein or vegetables in it.
recipes.tagline: The sauce base from the butter chicken.

instructions.title: Aromatics
instructions.body: Melt the butter over medium heat. Add the onion, garlic, and ginger and cook 4 to 5 minutes until soft.

instructions.title: Bloom spices
instructions.body: Stir in the tomato paste, garam masala, cumin, and paprika; cook 1 minute.

instructions.title: Simmer
instructions.body: Add the crushed tomatoes and salt; simmer 10 minutes. Blend smooth with an immersion blender if you like.

instructions.title: Finish
instructions.body: Stir in the cream and monk fruit and simmer 5 minutes. Add cooked chicken, other proteins, or vegetables to warm through.

recipes.notes: This is the sauce base from Butter Chicken. In the full recipe the chicken is first marinated in ½ cup yogurt, 1 tsp garam masala, and ½ tsp turmeric before searing. Naturally gluten-free; the watch-out is store-bought curry paste, which can hide gluten.

recipes.name: Lemon Dijon Vinaigrette
recipes.description: A bright lemon and dijon vinaigrette. The house dressing across several salads.
recipes.tagline: The lemon-dijon dressing shared by three salads.

instructions.title: Whisk
instructions.body: Whisk everything together until emulsified. Season with black pepper and dress greens just before serving.

recipes.notes: Use Maille or Grey Poupon dijon to keep it gluten-free. This one vinaigrette is used, with small tweaks, in three salads: Garlic Butter Shrimp over Arugula (as written here), Mixed Greens with Prosciutto (½ tsp dijon, no monk fruit, plus a pinch of pepper), and Kale Apple Goat Cheese (a sharper version with 3 tbsp oil, 1 tbsp + 1 tsp dijon, and 1 tbsp monk fruit).

recipes.name: Thai Lime Dressing
recipes.description: A punchy lime and fish sauce dressing with garlic and chili. Dress salads at the last minute.
recipes.tagline: The dressing from the Thai-style steak salad.

instructions.title: Whisk
instructions.body: Whisk everything together until the sweetener dissolves.

recipes.notes: This is the dressing from the Thai-Style Steak Salad. It contains fish sauce. Dress greens no more than about 5 minutes before serving so they stay crisp.

recipes.name: Sesame Lime Slaw Dressing
recipes.description: A lime and rice vinegar dressing with sesame. Made for slaws and crunchy vegetables.
recipes.tagline: The dressing from the mango slaw.

instructions.title: Whisk
instructions.body: Whisk everything together until the sweetener and salt dissolve. Add the red pepper flakes if you want heat.

recipes.notes: This is the dressing from the Mango Slaw. It is close to the Sesame Rice Vinegar Dressing but leads with lime.

recipes.name: Sesame Rice Vinegar Dressing
recipes.description: A simple rice vinegar and sesame dressing with a little heat. Great on cucumbers.
recipes.tagline: The dressing from the cucumber sesame salad.

instructions.title: Whisk
instructions.body: Whisk everything together until the sweetener and salt dissolve. Add the red pepper flakes if using.

recipes.notes: This is the dressing from the Cucumber Sesame Salad. It is the Sesame Lime Slaw Dressing without the lime, a touch sharper.
